commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: refactor(ETC-458): improve separation of concerns in RAG fallback logic
commits.body:
- Change execute_rag_call to return array|WP_Error instead of mixed
  {results, available} array, using the standard is_wp_error() pattern
- Add build_follow_up() to OpenAiPayloadBuilder to centralize follow-up
  payload construction (was hand-assembled inline in OpenAiProvider)
- Extract execute_rag_calls() loop from handle_function_calls, making it
  a thin orchestrator

Resolves CodeScene "Global Conditionals" warning by distributing
cyclomatic complexity across smaller, focused methods.

ETC-519.title: Remove custom block conversion logic
ETC-519.description:
- [ ] Remove legacy custom block migration logic  (pre-beta artifact)
- [ ] Remove legacy custom block conversion from and to gutenberg

ETC-458.title: Plugin: fall back to web search when RAG fails
ETC-458.description:
## Acceptance Criteria

- [X] Web search is not available to the AI when RAG returns results
- [X] Web search is available to the AI when `ENABLE_AI_RAG` is off
- [ ] Web search is not available when RAG returns valid response but no results
- [ ] Web search is available to the AI when RAG is unavailable

ETC-507.title: Add property data wrappers to gutenberg and etch.
ETC-507.description:
Add propert data attributes to let 3rd party devs identify certain property inputs.

Context:
[https://community.etchwp.com/c/developers-discussions/component-prop-meta-data-in-gutenberg-inspector-panel#comment_wrapper_98379826](<https://community.etchwp.com/c/developers-discussions/component-prop-meta-data-in-gutenberg-inspector-panel#comment_wrapper_98379826>)
